segments.text: India versus Pakistan. Biggest cities compared. Who wins?
Mega-city scale starts with Delhi and Mumbai, which together dwarf anything Pakistan can match.
Karachi and Lahore are huge cities, but India simply has more giant metros.
Airport traffic is another major difference. India moves far more passengers through its top hubs.
Metro rail footprint also leans heavily toward India.
Skylines, office districts, and urban GDP all point in the same direction.
Pakistan still has powerful urban centers with real regional importance.
But if the question is city scale and urban weight, India wins comfortably.
Pakistan has strong cities. India has a bigger urban universe.
